etymology.description: phonetic 其(其) (qí) + semantic 月(月) (yuè, "moon") -> changing moon marks passage of time -> period of time
etymology.interpretation: The character 期(qī, "period") combines the phonetic 其(其) (qí, "his") with the semantic 月(月) (yuè, "moon") to signify the phases of the moon, which naturally represent cycles and periods of time. The moon's regular changes provide a clear, cyclical marker of time's passage.

examples.chinese: 这个项目需要完成的时间是三个月(這個項目需要完成的時間是三個月)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù xūyào wánchéng de shíjiān shì sān gè yuè.
examples.english: The period required to complete this project is three months.

examples.chinese: 在考试期间(在考試期間)，请保持安静(請保持安靜)。
examples.pinyin: Zài kǎoshì qījiān, qǐng bǎochí ānjìng.
examples.english: During the exam period, please remain quiet.

examples.chinese: 我们在下一个阶段继续努力(我們在下一個階段繼續努力)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài xià yī gè jiēduàn jìxù nǔlì.
examples.english: We will continue to work hard in the next phase.
